ModuleName: Hypothesis Testing, Correlation, and Regression Analysis

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's learning topics, covering hypothesis testing foundations, p-values, A/B testing, correlation analysis, and regression, used by instructors to plan lessons and by learners to preview what the module addresses.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: Outlines the key topics covered in the module—hypothesis testing foundations, correlation, and regression analysis—serving as a navigational overview that helps learners and instructors understand the scope and sequence of concepts to be studied.

Artifacts.Name: Hypothesis_Testing_Correlati_generated.pptx
Artifacts.Description: A slide deck introducing inferential statistics concepts including null and alternative hypotheses, p-values, A/B testing design, correlation, and regression analysis, used by instructors to deliver lectures or by learners to review core concepts and definitions.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations of Hypothesis Testing
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces statistical inference and hypothesis testing fundamentals — including null and alternative hypotheses, Type I and Type II errors, and the logic of sampling — serving as the conceptual foundation learners need before conducting any formal statistical test.

Artifacts.Name: P-Values and Statistical Significance
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains what p-values actually measure, how to interpret statistical significance correctly, and common misconceptions surrounding both concepts, helping learners and instructors ground hypothesis testing decisions in sound reasoning rather than mechanical thresholds.

Artifacts.Name: A/B Testing in Practice
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers how to design, run, and interpret A/B tests in real-world settings — including defining metrics, randomizing treatment groups, and avoiding common pitfalls — giving learners a practical framework for applying hypothesis testing to product and marketing decisions.

Artifacts.Name: Correlation Analysis
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to measure and interpret the relationship between two variables using correlation coefficients such as Pearson's r, addresses the distinction between correlation and causation, and guides learners on when and how to apply correlation analysis appropriately.

Artifacts.Name: Simple Linear Regression
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains how to build and interpret a simple linear regression model — covering the least-squares line, slope and intercept interpretation, R-squared, and residual analysis — so learners can quantify and predict the relationship between one predictor and one outcome variable.

Artifacts.Name: Multiple Linear Regression
Artifacts.Description: This reading extends regression to models with multiple predictor variables, covering coefficient interpretation, multicollinearity, model fit assessment, and variable selection, enabling learners to analyze and predict outcomes influenced by several factors simultaneously.

Artifacts.Name: Drawing Data-Driven Conclusions
Artifacts.Description: This reading synthesizes the module by explaining how to translate statistical outputs from hypothesis tests, correlation, and regression into sound, defensible conclusions, guiding learners on validating assumptions, avoiding overinterpretation, and communicating findings responsibly.

Artifacts.Name: Quiz Question Bank (20 questions)
Artifacts.Description: A 20-question quiz bank assessing learners' understanding of hypothesis testing fundamentals such as statistical inference, null hypotheses, test statistics, Type I errors, and p-value interpretation, used by instructors to build graded assessments or practice quizzes.
